title: The Broken Definition of Strength That's Quietly Destroying Men
description:
We inherited a definition of strength that was wrong from the start — and nobody told us. This video is about mental health stigma, what silence is actually costing, and why the personal trainer analogy changes everything.

I spent years white-knuckling life — passive-aggressive in meetings, snapping at people I cared about, awake at 3 AM running mental loops I couldn't switch off. I told myself that was the cost of ambition. It wasn't. It was a person who needed help and had no framework for asking.

In this video I go through why we'll admit a sprained ankle before we admit we're drowning, what actually changes when someone stops pretending they have it handled, and what breaking the silence looks like at the level most of us actually operate at.

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

[LINKS_PLACEHOLDER]

If this landed somewhere, leave a comment — a moment when someone made space for you, or when you made space for someone else. The small ones count too.
thumbnail_concept: Split frame: left side shows a smiling group of people surrounding someone with an arm cast, warm light, full social permission — right side shows the same person alone at a table, muted tones, chest-level frame, looking down and unreachable. Bold white text overlaid on the right side: 'We don't talk about this kind.' Cold vs warm color temperature sharpens the contrast.
end_screen_suggestion: Surface the Life & Self-Development playlist or a related video on navigating burnout or building emotional resilience.
